Flow.description: Autolaunched Flow wrapping ForecastScenariosActionHandler. Runs three Monte Carlo simulations to produce Commit, Most Likely, and Best Case forecast tiers against the current user's open Opportunities. Returns per-tier probabilities, expected revenues, and a combined narrative summary.
Flow.label: Run Forecast Scenarios (Commit / Most Likely / Best Case)

variables.description: Optional. If the user mentions a specific dollar target (e.g. "What is my commit vs. best case for $25M?"), enter it here as a number like 25000000. The output will then include probability of hitting that target for each tier. If the user just asks "what is my commit?" or "show me my forecast scenarios" with no target mentioned, leave this blank — the summary will show expected revenues for each tier instead.
variables.name: revenueTarget

variables.description: The number of days from today to include opportunities closing within. Leave blank when the user says "this quarter" or does not specify a time period — the action will automatically calculate the days remaining in the current calendar quarter. Only populate this if the user specifies a custom window such as "next 30 days" or "next 60 days."
variables.name: timeHorizonDays

variables.description: A combined plain-English narrative comparing all three forecast tiers (Commit, Most Likely, Best Case). Read this response directly to the user — it already contains probability percentages, expected revenues, and a recommendation.
variables.name: overallSummary

variables.description: True if all scenario simulations completed successfully. If false, do not read the forecast results — instead read errorMessage to the user.
variables.name: success

variables.description: A description of what went wrong if success is false. Read this to the user so they understand why the forecast failed.
variables.name: errorMessage

variables.description: Probability of hitting the revenue target using only deals with 80% or higher probability (your committed, high-confidence pipeline). Example: "72.4%".
variables.name: commitProbabilityPct

variables.description: Mean revenue outcome in the Commit scenario (deals with >=80% probability), in USD.
variables.name: commitExpectedRevenue

variables.description: 10th percentile revenue outcome in the Commit scenario — the pessimistic floor for committed deals, in USD.
variables.name: commitP10Revenue

variables.description: 90th percentile revenue outcome in the Commit scenario — the optimistic ceiling for committed deals, in USD.
variables.name: commitP90Revenue

variables.description: Number of deals qualifying for the Commit scenario (those with probability >= 80%).
variables.name: commitOppsCount

variables.description: Probability of hitting the revenue target including all deals with 50% or higher probability (your likely pipeline). Example: "58.9%".
variables.name: mostLikelyProbabilityPct

variables.description: Mean revenue outcome in the Most Likely scenario (deals with >=50% probability), in USD.
variables.name: mostLikelyExpectedRevenue

variables.description: 10th percentile revenue outcome in the Most Likely scenario — pessimistic floor, in USD.
variables.name: mostLikelyP10Revenue

variables.description: 90th percentile revenue outcome in the Most Likely scenario — optimistic ceiling, in USD.
variables.name: mostLikelyP90Revenue

variables.description: Number of deals qualifying for the Most Likely scenario (those with probability >= 50%).
variables.name: mostLikelyOppsCount

variables.description: Probability of hitting the revenue target including ALL open deals regardless of probability (the most optimistic scenario). Example: "81.2%".
variables.name: bestCaseProbabilityPct

variables.description: Mean revenue outcome in the Best Case scenario (all open deals), in USD.
variables.name: bestCaseExpectedRevenue

variables.description: 10th percentile revenue outcome in the Best Case scenario — pessimistic floor, in USD.
variables.name: bestCaseP10Revenue

variables.description: 90th percentile revenue outcome in the Best Case scenario — optimistic ceiling, in USD.
variables.name: bestCaseP90Revenue

variables.description: Total number of open deals included in the Best Case scenario (all open opportunities regardless of probability).
variables.name: bestCaseOppsCount
